mnemonics: MAR: Memory Address Register Holds the 'Where'
Every Byte Gets Its Own Mailbox (Address)
Data Bus is a Two-Way Street (Bi-Directional)
keyPoints: Each memory location has a unique numerical address.
The size of the address bus determines the maximum addressable memory (e.g., 32-bit bus = 4GB).
The Memory Address Register (MAR) holds the address for the next read/write operation.
commonMistakes: Confusing memory addresses with the data stored at those addresses.
Forgetting that the address bus is one-way (CPU to memory) while the data bus is two-way.
Miscalculating total addressable memory (e.g., 2^n, where n is the number of address lines).
